## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` against a canonical-established depot.
  
  ## Iteration loop
  
  Each turn:
  
  1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
  2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
  3. Recommend a fix.
  4. Await guidance.
  
  No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.
  
  ## In-flight progress
  
  Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:
  
  1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED
  2. `kludge-tadmor` PASSED — first green confirmation of the kludge/ordain self-veto fixes (`142cf920` + `c547c62a`).
  3. `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED.
  
  Cases not yet observed in this run: `conjure-srjcl`, `ordain-airgap`, `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Next mount: re-run from the top against still-valid canonical depot+SAs (case probes will gate); if precondition state still holds, advance through the remaining cases and diagnose the first new failure.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  
  Green tally on onboarding-sequence.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - BBAAu landed.
  - canonical-establish (BBAAe) for depot+SAs.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Construction — BBAAu.
  - Full gauntlet — BBAAX.
  - moriah Fixture static — phantom.

48abbe1e  ₢BBAAs    fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.                
8c057a45  ₢BBAAs    BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). Verification: bash -n green on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4 baseline); shellcheck warnings on both files are all pre-existing (kindle vars consumed cross-module, $(<file) on local declarations) — no new warnings from this notch.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
33798bfc  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36. Verification: bash -n green on both edited files; full re-run pending after operator restarts the chat in clean pace remount. Empirical onboarding-sequence run surfaced HTTP 400 'key _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n in the substitution data is not matched in the template' on the very first ordain (conjure of rbev-sentry-deb-tether). Direct inspection of the rejected build payload (rbfd_context_build.json) showed the four GAR/RELIQUARY substitutions referenced as ${_KEY} bare in the script body — accepted by Cloud Build — and the three ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n substitutions referenced as ${_KEY:-} (bash default-value form) — rejected. Only structural difference between matched and unmatched. The runtime guard one line below (test -n LOCATOR || continue) already handles the empty-string case for non-conjure modes, so the :- defaults were doing no semantic work. This was the BBAA3 wrap-criterion run that dca68895 explicitly deferred to BBAAs; the bug was guaranteed to surface on first empirical submission. 48abbe1e  ₢BBAAs    fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.                
8c057a45  ₢BBAAs    BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). 33798bfc  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36. Verification: bash -n green on both edited files; full re-run pending after operator restarts the chat in clean pace remount. 9ea3b9fa  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight script: drop bash default-value syntax from the three enshrine-locator reads so Cloud Build's automapSubstitutions scanner recognizes them as references. Empirical onboarding-sequence run surfaced HTTP 400 'key _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n in the substitution data is not matched in the template' on the very first ordain (conjure of rbev-sentry-deb-tether). Direct inspection of the rejected build payload (rbfd_context_build.json) showed the four GAR/RELIQUARY substitutions referenced as ${_KEY} bare in the script body — accepted by Cloud Build — and the three ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n substitutions referenced as ${_KEY:-} (bash default-value form) — rejected. Only structural difference between matched and unmatched. The runtime guard one line below (test -n LOCATOR || continue) already handles the empty-string case for non-conjure modes, so the :- defaults were doing no semantic work. This was the BBAA3 wrap-criterion run that dca68895 explicitly deferred to BBAAs; the bug was guaranteed to surface on first empirical submission. The strings are runtime-built commit subjects — 'BBAAu kludge-{}: sentry hallmark', 'BBAAu inscribe-reliquary: ...', 'BBAA0 ordain-airgap: ...', etc. — which embedded the coronet identifier of the pace that originally landed each function as a static string literal in source. content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced

151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

## Character

## Iteration loop

Each turn:

1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
3. Recommend a fix.
4. Await guidance.

No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.

## In-flight progress

Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:

1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED

content.text:
Different failure mode than expected — the BBAAs off-by-one site never gets reached. Three diagnostic facts:

```
WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge
ERROR: [rbfd_enshrine] Vessel argument required (sigil or path)
```

So:
- The orchestrator invokes `tt/rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge`
- The workbench dispatcher rejects the argument (channel says no folio)
- The underlying enshrine handler then dies because it needs a vessel arg

Contract mismatch between the tabtarget enrollment (no folio) and what enshrine requires (vessel sigil). Investigating the call site and the enrollment.

311	/// Enshrine one vessel — mirrors that vessel's RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN bases
312	/// to GAR and writes back the resulting RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR digests.
313	/// `rbfd_enshrine` is per-vessel; the airgap chain calls this twice with
314	/// different vessels (forge first, then airgap-bottle).
315	fn rbtdro_enshrine(
316	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
317	    dir: &Path,
318	    vessel_sigil: &str,
319	    label: &str,
320	) -> Result<(), rbtdre_Verdict> {
321	    let result = match rbtdro_invoke_logged(
322	        ctx,
323	        RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ENSHRINE_VESSEL,
324	        &[vessel_sigil],
325	        &[],
326	        dir,
327	        label,
328	    ) {
329	        Ok(r) => r,
330	        Err(e) => return Err(rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("enshrine invocation: {}", e))),
331	    };
332	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
333	        return Err(rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
334	            "enshrine {} exit {}\n{}",
335	            vessel_sigil, result.exit_code, result.stderr
336	        )));
337	    }
338	    Ok(())
339	}
340	
341	/// Pull a docker image. Used by the graft case to ensure the source image
342	/// is locally resolvable before the BURE_TWEAK-overridden ordain.
343	fn rbtdro_docker_pull(image_ref: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
344	    let output = std::process::Command::new("docker")
345	        .args(["pull", image_ref])
346	        .output()
347	        .map_err(|e| format!("docker pull exec failed: {}", e))?;
348	    if !output.status.success() {
349	        return Err(format!(
350	            "docker pull {} exited {}: {}",
351	            image_ref,
352	            output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
353	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).trim()
354	        ));

845	rbfd_enshrine() {
846	  zrbfd_sentinel
847	
848	  buc_doc_brief "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
849	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
850	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
851	
852	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load
853	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
854	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
855	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
856	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
857	  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "conjure" \
858	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a conjure vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"
859	
860	  # Check at least one slot declares an upstream ORIGIN. ANCHOR is a derived
861	  # value computed by this operation from ORIGIN; a vessel with no ORIGIN has
862	  # nothing for enshrine to mirror.
863	  local z_has_origin=false
864	  local z_n=""
865	  local z_origin_var=""
866	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
867	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
868	    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_origin=true
869	  done
870	  test "${z_has_origin}" = "true" \
871	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
872	
873	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)

rbfd_enshrine() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a conjure vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"

  # Check at least one slot declares an upstream ORIGIN. ANCHOR is a derived
  # value computed by this operation from ORIGIN; a vessel with no ORIGIN has
  # nothing for enshrine to mirror.
  local z_has_origin=false
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_origin=true
  done
  test "${z_has_origin}" = "true" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

local z_base_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_base_dir}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: base_dir required"
  shift 2

  # Find colophon in registry
  local z_found=""
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
    z_found="${z_i}"
    break
  done
  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon not found: ${z_colophon}"

  # Decode folio from channel
  local z_folio=""
  local z_args=("$@")
  case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
    "")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        buc_warn "Colophon '${z_colophon}' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): ${z_args[*]}"
        z_args=()
      fi
      ;;
    "imprint")
      z_folio="${BURD_TOKEN_3}"
      ;;
    "param1")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        z_folio="${z_args[0]}"
        z_args=("${z_args[@]:1}")
      fi
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
      ;;
  esac

  # Dispatch with folio in exec environment

source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfc_kindle
  zrbfl_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified vessel.
  # zrbrv_enforce intentionally skipped — yoke (the only rbfl_* command with
  # param1 channel today) writes RBRV_RELIQUARY (kindle constant, min=1);
  # pre-write enforce self-vetoes on marshal-zero state. rbw-rvv
  # (rbrv_cli.sh:108) is the strict validator path.
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local z_vessel_file="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/rbrv.env"
    test -f "${z_vessel_file}" || buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_vessel_file}"
    source "${z_vessel_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source vessel: ${z_vessel_file}"
    zrbrv_kindle
  fi
}

buc_execute rbfl_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger" zrbfl_furnish "$@"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbfd_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided temporary directory, empty at start of command"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_NOW_STAMP        " "Bash Dispatch Utility provided string unique between invocations"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgl_GarLayout.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate BUD environment'
  zburd_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Validate required tools'
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "git not found - required for assuring controlled build context"

  buc_log_args 'Container runtime settings'
  RBG_RUNTIME="${RBG_RUNTIME:-podman}"
  RBG_RUNTIME_ARG="${RBG_RUNTIME_ARG:-}"

  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrv_regime.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle modules in dependency order'
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgl_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbfd_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbfd_ "Recipe Bottle Foundry Director Build" zrbfd_furnish "$@"

file.content:
rbfd_ordain() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain a hallmark from a vessel (conjure, mirror, or graft based on vessel mode)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Peek at vessel mode without sourcing (sourcing makes vars readonly,
  # and the downstream function will source again via zrbfc_load_vessel)
  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  local z_mode=""
  local z_mode_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_mode_line || test -n "${z_mode_line}"; do
    case "${z_mode_line}" in
      RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=*) z_mode="${z_mode_line#RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=}"; break ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
  z_mode="${z_mode:-conjure}"
  case "${z_mode}" in
    conjure) rbfd_build "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;

zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle
zbuz_kindle
zrbz_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
rbw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "RBWSHOW: $*"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

rbw_route() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  rbw_show "Routing command: ${z_command} with args: $*"

  zburd_sentinel
  zrbz_healthcheck

  rbw_show "BURD environment verified"

  # Qualification gate for commands that need it
  case "${z_command}" in
    "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}"|"${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}")
      "${RBW_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbq_cli.sh" rbq_qualify_fast || buc_die "Qualification gate failed"
      ;;
  esac

  buz_exec_lookup "${z_command}" "${RBW_SCRIPT_DIR}" "$@"
}

rbw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

# Helper function to delegate to BURD
# Usage: bul_launch "path/to/workbench.sh" "$@"
bul_launch() {
  local z_coordinator="$1"
  shift

  # Detect terminal width via /dev/tty (survives exec chain and dispatch pipes)
  # Subshell probe: /dev/tty may exist but not be openable (CI, sandbox)
  BURD_TERM_COLS=80
  if (exec </dev/tty) 2>/dev/null; then
    read -r _ BURD_TERM_COLS < <(stty size </dev/tty 2>/dev/null)
    test -n "${BURD_TERM_COLS}" || BURD_TERM_COLS=80
  fi
  export BURD_TERM_COLS

  export BURD_COORDINATOR_SCRIPT="${z_coordinator}"
  exec "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/buk/bud_dispatch.sh" "${1##*/}" "${@:2}"
}

zbud_main() {
  zbud_show "Starting BDU dispatch"

  # Decide color policy before stdout is piped
  zbud_resolve_color

  # Setup environment
  zbud_setup || { echo "ERROR: Environment setup failed" >&2; exit 1; }
  zbud_show "Environment setup complete"

  # Source fact-file module for BURX writes
  source "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/buk/buf_fact.sh"

  # Process arguments
  zbud_process_args "$@" || { echo "ERROR: Argument processing failed" >&2; exit 1; }
  zbud_show "Arguments processed"

  # Write initial BURX exchange state
  zbud_write_burx_initial

  # Detect unexpected BURD_ variables
  local -r z_known="BURD_CONFIG_DIR BURD_REGIME_FILE BURD_NO_LOG BURD_INTERACTIVE BURD_COORDINATOR_SCRIPT BURD_LAUNCHER BURD_STATION_FILE BURD_TERM_COLS BURD_NOW_STAMP BURD_NOW_EPOCH BURD_TEMP_DIR BURD_OUTPUT_DIR BURD_TRANSCRIPT BURD_GIT_CONTEXT BURD_LOG_LAST BURD_LOG_SAME BURD_LOG_HIST BURD_COMMAND BURD_TARGET BURD_CLI_ARGS BURD_TOKEN_1 BURD_TOKEN_2 BURD_TOKEN_3 BURD_TOKEN_4 BURD_TOKEN_5 BURD_TOOLS_DIR BURD_BUK_DIR BURD_TABTARGET_DIR"
  ZBURD_UNEXPECTED=()
  local z_var
  for z_var in $(compgen -v BURD_); do
    case " ${z_known} " in
      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
      *) ZBURD_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    esac
  done

  # Die on unexpected variables
  if test ${#ZBURD_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
    zbud_die "Unexpected BURD_ variables: ${ZBURD_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
  fi

  ZBURD_INITIALIZED=1

  # Build complete invocation array (always has ≥2 elements, so always safe under set -u)
  local -r z_coordinator_cmd="${BURD_COORDINATOR_SCRIPT}"
  local -a z_invocation=("${z_coordinator_cmd}" "${BURD_COMMAND}")
  if test ${#BURD_CLI_ARGS[@]} -gt 0; then
    z_invocation+=("${BURD_CLI_ARGS[@]}")
  fi
  zbud_show "Coordinator command: ${z_invocation[*]}"

  # Log command to all log files (or suppress all output if BURD_NO_LOG)
  if test -z "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then
    if test -n "${BURD_INTERACTIVE:-}"; then
      echo "log (interactive): ${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
      echo "command: ${z_invocation[*]}" >> "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
      echo "Git context: ${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"  >> "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
    else
      echo "log files:   ${BURD_LOG_LAST} ${BURD_LOG_SAME} ${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
      echo "command: ${z_invocation[*]}" >> "${BURD_LOG_LAST}"
      echo "command: ${z_invocation[*]}" >> "${BURD_LOG_SAME}"
      echo "command: ${z_invocation[*]}" >> "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
      echo "Git context: ${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"  >> "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
    fi
    echo "transcript:  ${BURD_TRANSCRIPT}"
    echo "output dir:  ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"
  fi

  zbud_show "Executing coordinator"

  # Execute coordinator with logging
  set +e
  zBURD_STATUS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/status-$$"
  if test -n "${BURD_INTERACTIVE:-}"; then
    # Interactive mode: uncurated logging to historical log, preserves line buffering
    "${z_invocation[@]}" 2>&1 | tee -a "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
    zBURD_EXIT_STATUS=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    echo "${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}" > "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}"
    zbud_show "Coordinator status (interactive): ${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}"
  elif test -n "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then
    {
      "${z_invocation[@]}"
      echo $? > "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}"
      zbud_show "Coordinator status: $(cat "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}")"
    }
  else
    {
      "${z_invocation[@]}" 2>&1
      echo $? > "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}"
      zbud_show "Coordinator status: $(cat "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}")"
    } | while IFS= read -r z_line; do
        printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${BURD_LOG_LAST}"
        printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" | zbud_curate_same >> "${BURD_LOG_SAME}"
        printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" | zbud_curate_hist >> "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
        printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"  # to stdout
      done
  fi

  zBURD_EXIT_STATUS=$(cat "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}")
  rm                     "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}"

  # Write BURX completion state
  zbud_write_burx_completion "${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}"

  set -e

  # Generate checksum for the log files (only when enabled)
  if test -z "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then
    zbud_generate_checksum "${BURD_LOG_SAME}" "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
    zbud_show "Checksum generated"
  fi

  zbud_show "BDU completed with status: ${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}"

  exit "${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}"
}

zbud_main "$@"

# eof

232	/// verdict-bearing Err so callers can early-return cleanly.
233	fn rbtdro_ordain_capture(
234	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
235	    dir: &Path,
236	    vessel_dir: &str,
237	    extra_env: &[(&str, &str)],
238	    label: &str,

248	        Ok(r) => r,
249	        Err(e) => {

251	                "ordain {} invocation: {}",

253	            )))
254	        }
255	    };

270	    };

zbud_process_args() {
  local -r z_target="${1}"
  shift

  zbud_show "Processing target: ${z_target}"

  # Extract tokens from tabtarget
  local -a z_tokens
  IFS="${BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER}" read -ra z_tokens <<< "${z_target}"
  zbud_show "Split tokens: ${z_tokens[*]}"

  # Store primary command token (legacy, equivalent to BURD_TOKEN_1)
  BURD_COMMAND="${z_tokens[0]}"

  # Explode tokens into numbered variables for workbench access
  # Pattern matches MBC_TTPARAM__FIRST through MBC_TTPARAM__FIFTH
  BURD_TOKEN_1="${z_tokens[0]:-}"
  BURD_TOKEN_2="${z_tokens[1]:-}"
  BURD_TOKEN_3="${z_tokens[2]:-}"
  BURD_TOKEN_4="${z_tokens[3]:-}"
  BURD_TOKEN_5="${z_tokens[4]:-}"

  export BURD_TOKEN_1 BURD_TOKEN_2 BURD_TOKEN_3 BURD_TOKEN_4 BURD_TOKEN_5

  # Create tag for log files
  local -r z_tag="${z_tokens[0]}-${z_tokens[2]:-unknown}"

  # Setup log paths and files (skip entirely for no-log tabtargets)
  if test -z "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then
    BURD_LOG_LAST="${BURS_LOG_DIR}/${BURC_LOG_LAST}.${BURC_LOG_EXT}"
    BURD_LOG_SAME="${BURS_LOG_DIR}/same-${z_tag}.${BURC_LOG_EXT}"
    BURD_LOG_HIST="${BURS_LOG_DIR}/hist-${z_tag}-${BURD_NOW_STAMP}.${BURC_LOG_EXT}"
    mkdir -p "${BURS_LOG_DIR}"
    : > "${BURD_LOG_LAST}"
    : > "${BURD_LOG_SAME}"
    : > "${BURD_LOG_HIST}"
  fi

  # Store target and extra arguments
  BURD_TARGET="${z_target}"
  BURD_CLI_ARGS=("$@")

  # Export command context for workbench access
  export BURD_COMMAND
  export BURD_TARGET

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBW Workbench - Routes Recipe Bottle commands to CLIs via zipper registry
#
# All commands dispatch via buz_exec_lookup (see rbz_zipper.sh for colophon mapping).
# Qualification gate runs for rbw-cC (crucible charge) and rbw-fO (hallmark ordain) before dispatch.

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory
RBW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
source "${RBW_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbz_zipper.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Kindle dispatch and zipper registry
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle
zbuz_kindle
zrbz_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
rbw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "RBWSHOW: $*"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

rbw_route() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  rbw_show "Routing command: ${z_command} with args: $*"

  zburd_sentinel
  zrbz_healthcheck

  rbw_show "BURD environment verified"

  # Qualification gate for commands that need it
  case "${z_command}" in
    "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}"|"${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}")
      "${RBW_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbq_cli.sh" rbq_qualify_fast || buc_die "Qualification gate failed"
      ;;
  esac

  buz_exec_lookup "${z_command}" "${RBW_SCRIPT_DIR}" "$@"
}

rbw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

  rbw_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

rbw_main "$@"

# eof

100	
101	# buz_enroll() - Register colophon tuple in parallel rolls
102	# Args: varname, colophon, module, command, channel, description
103	# All 6 arguments required. Channel: "" (none), "imprint", or "param1".
104	# Assigns colophon string to caller's variable via printf -v
105	# Side effects: populates registry rolls (must be called in same process, NOT inside $())
106	buz_enroll() {
107	  zbuz_sentinel
108	
109	  test $# -eq 6 || buc_die "buz_enroll: requires 6 arguments (varname colophon module command channel description), got $#"
110	
111	  local -r z_varname="${1}"
112	  local -r z_colophon="${2}"
113	  local -r z_module="${3}"
114	  local -r z_command="${4}"
115	  local -r z_channel="${5}"
116	  local -r z_description="${6}"
117	  test -n "${z_varname}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: varname required"
118	  test -n "${z_colophon}"    || buc_die "buz_enroll: colophon required"
119	  test -n "${z_module}"      || buc_die "buz_enroll: module required"
120	  test -n "${z_command}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: command required"
121	  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: description required"
122	
123	  # Validate channel value (empty string is valid — means no channel)
124	  case "${z_channel}" in
125	    ""|"imprint"|"param1") ;;
126	    *) buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid channel: ${z_channel}" ;;
127	  esac
128	
129	  # Validate variable name
130	  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
131	    || buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid variable name: ${z_varname}"
132	
133	  # Roll population (only persists in same-process context, lost in $() subshell)
134	  z_buz_colophon_roll+=("${z_colophon}")
135	  z_buz_module_roll+=("${z_module}")
136	  z_buz_command_roll+=("${z_command}")
137	  z_buz_channel_roll+=("${z_channel}")
138	  z_buz_describe_roll+=("${z_description}")
139	
140	  # Assign colophon to caller's variable
141	  printf -v "${z_varname}" '%s' "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: printf -v failed for ${z_varname}"
142	}
143	
144	######################################################################
145	# Context generation (emit markdown from registry metadata)
146	
147	# buz_emit_context() - Emit colophon registry as markdown, organized by groups
148	# Args: tabtarget_dir
149	# Writes markdown to stdout. Groups come from buz_group; descriptions from buz_enroll.
150	# Frontispiece extracted from tabtarget filenames on disk.
151	buz_emit_context() {
152	  zbuz_sentinel
153	
154	  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
155	  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "buz_emit_context: tabtarget directory required"
156	
157	  printf '%s\n' "## Command Reference (Generated)"
158	  printf '%s\n' ""
159	  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->"

# buz_enroll() - Register colophon tuple in parallel rolls
# Args: varname, colophon, module, command, channel, description
# All 6 arguments required. Channel: "" (none), "imprint", or "param1".
# Assigns colophon string to caller's variable via printf -v
# Side effects: populates registry rolls (must be called in same process, NOT inside $())
buz_enroll() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  test $# -eq 6 || buc_die "buz_enroll: requires 6 arguments (varname colophon module command channel description), got $#"

  local -r z_varname="${1}"
  local -r z_colophon="${2}"
  local -r z_module="${3}"
  local -r z_command="${4}"
  local -r z_channel="${5}"
  local -r z_description="${6}"
  test -n "${z_varname}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: varname required"
  test -n "${z_colophon}"    || buc_die "buz_enroll: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_module}"      || buc_die "buz_enroll: module required"
  test -n "${z_command}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: command required"
  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: description required"

  # Validate channel value (empty string is valid — means no channel)
  case "${z_channel}" in
    ""|"imprint"|"param1") ;;
    *) buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid channel: ${z_channel}" ;;
  esac

  # Validate variable name
  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
    || buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid variable name: ${z_varname}"

  # Roll population (only persists in same-process context, lost in $() subshell)
  z_buz_colophon_roll+=("${z_colophon}")
  z_buz_module_roll+=("${z_module}")
  z_buz_command_roll+=("${z_command}")
  z_buz_channel_roll+=("${z_channel}")
  z_buz_describe_roll+=("${z_description}")

  # Assign colophon to caller's variable
  printf -v "${z_varname}" '%s' "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: printf -v failed for ${z_varname}"
}

######################################################################
# Context generation (emit markdown from registry metadata)

# buz_emit_context() - Emit colophon registry as markdown, organized by groups
# Args: tabtarget_dir
# Writes markdown to stdout. Groups come from buz_group; descriptions from buz_enroll.
# Frontispiece extracted from tabtarget filenames on disk.
buz_emit_context() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "buz_emit_context: tabtarget directory required"

  printf '%s\n' "## Command Reference (Generated)"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->"
